evals.name: notification-center-webapp
evals.prompt: Ich will ein Notification Center implementieren für meine SaaS-App. Mach mir einen Design-Brief.
evals.expected_output: A structured design brief markdown file in docs/design/ with downloaded reference screenshots in docs/design/screenshots/. Should include pattern recommendations, UI details, UX principles table, and anti-patterns for notification center design.

assertions.text: A markdown design brief file exists in docs/design/
At least 3 screenshot image files exist in docs/design/screenshots/ with non-trivial file size (>5KB)
The brief contains a TL;DR or summary section with at least 3 concrete recommendations
The brief analyses at least 3 real-world apps or sources (not just general advice)
The brief references specific UX principles or laws (e.g. Jakob's Law, Hick's Law, NN/g)
The brief includes a section on what to avoid / anti-patterns
The brief specifically addresses web/SaaS context
Recommendations are concrete and actionable rather than vague
At least 2 ASCII wireframe .txt files exist in docs/design/wireframes/ and contain box-drawing characters (┌, │, └, ─) showing actual UI layout

evals.name: tower-defense-upgrade-shop-game
evals.prompt: Ich baue gerade ein mobiles Phaser-Game, so ein Tower-Defense-Ding. Ich brauche ein Upgrade-System wo Nutzer zwischen Runden ihre Türme upgraden können. Irgendwie so ein Shop-Screen. Wie sollte das designt sein?
evals.expected_output: A structured design brief with game-UI-specific recommendations, screenshots from game UI databases, and Phaser/mobile-game-specific UX guidance.

assertions.text: A markdown design brief or design document file exists in docs/design/
At least 3 screenshot image files exist in docs/design/screenshots/ with non-trivial file size (>5KB)
The brief references at least 3 actual games (e.g. Kingdom Rush, Clash Royale, Brawl Stars, BTD6) as design references
The brief includes Phaser-specific or mobile game-specific implementation advice
The brief addresses the between-rounds time pressure context and its implications
The brief addresses touch target sizes or mobile interaction constraints
Recommendations are concrete and actionable rather than vague
At least 2 ASCII wireframe .txt files exist in docs/design/wireframes/ and contain box-drawing characters (┌, │, └, ─) showing actual UI layout

evals.name: inapp-chatbot-support-webapp
evals.prompt: Ich implementiere einen In-App-Chatbot für User-Support in meiner Web-App. Die Nutzer sollen Fragen stellen können und der Bot antwortet. Manchmal soll man auch an einen Menschen weiterleiten können. Schau mal wie das andere lösen und mach mir ein Design-Brief.
evals.expected_output: A structured design brief with chatbot UI pattern recommendations, human handoff UX patterns, reference screenshots, and mobile-aware layout recommendations.

assertions.text: A markdown design brief file exists in docs/design/
At least 3 screenshot image files exist in docs/design/screenshots/ with non-trivial file size (>5KB)
The brief references at least 3 real products (e.g. Intercom, Zendesk, Crisp, Tidio) as design references
The brief has dedicated coverage of the human handoff UX pattern
The brief references specific UX principles or psychology patterns relevant to chat UI
The brief covers chat widget placement, dimensions or layout decisions
Recommendations are concrete and actionable rather than vague
At least 2 ASCII wireframe .txt files exist in docs/design/wireframes/ and contain box-drawing characters (┌, │, └, ─) showing actual UI layout
